heading: Local MR: main → e524ea0 (initial commit)
title: Local MR: main → e524ea0 (initial commit)

focusedCommit.label: Human review index
focusedCommit.title: Review the MR in human order
focusedCommit.description: Overview · docs-first, then dependency and risk order. Six broad steps reveal the architecture before implementation detail.
reviewFocus: V2 · Map the CLI and agent contract
V3 · Verify freezing, materialization, and storage
V4 · Follow the review runtime into the browser

commits.subject: Read the contract before the implementation
commits.body: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.description: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.intent: Start with the human-facing explanation of why Virtual Commits exist, how reviewers use them, and which invariants the implementation must preserve.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Extract the source-freezing, block-conservation, and final-tree guarantees before reading their implementation.
Check that the concise README workflow agrees with the detailed guide.

risk.reason: The documentation defines the reviewer's mental model and must not promise guarantees the code does not enforce.

commits.subject: Map the CLI and agent contract
commits.body: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.description: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.intent: Follow the user and agent entry points from source capture through manifest submission and structured validation.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Trace manifest input from the CLI boundary through schema and block-assignment validation.
Confirm the bundled Skill describes the same protocol and safety boundary enforced by the CLI.

risk.reason: This contract decides which frozen source an agent may reorganize and which invalid plans are rejected.

commits.subject: Verify freezing, materialization, and storage
commits.body: Review the deterministic core that freezes the real Diff, assigns every change block once, materializes cumulative states, and stores immutable revisions.
commits.description: Review the deterministic core that freezes the real Diff, assigns every change block once, materializes cumulative states, and stores immutable revisions.
commits.intent: Review the deterministic core that freezes the real Diff, assigns every change block once, materializes cumulative states, and stores immutable revisions.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check merge-base selection and stable block identities across every supported change kind.
Verify exact block conservation, final-tree equality, atomic persistence, and private file permissions.

risk.reason: A flaw here could omit code, compare the wrong source, or make a supposedly immutable review revision drift.

commits.subject: Follow the review runtime into the browser
commits.body: Connect saved revisions to Real and Virtual navigation, Diff rendering, context expansion, previews, and browser state.
commits.description: Connect saved revisions to Real and Virtual navigation, Diff rendering, context expansion, previews, and browser state.
commits.intent: Connect saved revisions to Real and Virtual navigation, Diff rendering, context expansion, previews, and browser state.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Follow one frozen revision through discovery, runtime identity checks, server responses, and browser restoration.
Confirm Real and Virtual selections use the shared workspace without displaying stale comparison data.

risk.reason: Correct stored data is still unsafe to review if the browser presents a stale or mismatched comparison.

commits.subject: Challenge the guarantees with tests
commits.body: Match the implementation's safety claims to adversarial unit, integration, installation, and browser regressions.
commits.description: Match the implementation's safety claims to adversarial unit, integration, installation, and browser regressions.
commits.intent: Match the implementation's safety claims to adversarial unit, integration, installation, and browser regressions.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Look for missing and duplicate blocks, repository drift, target merges, binary changes, renames, and concurrent revisions.
Confirm browser coverage exercises source switching and context expansion rather than only static markup.

risk.reason: The tests are the executable evidence that reordering changes presentation rather than content.

commits.subject: Finish with release metadata
commits.body: Review package wiring and change notes after the implementation and its evidence are understood.
commits.description: Review package wiring and change notes after the implementation and its evidence are understood.
commits.intent: Review package wiring and change notes after the implementation and its evidence are understood.
commits.author: Human review order

reviewFocus.text: Check that package entry points and scripts expose the documented workflow.
Confirm both change logs describe the same shipped capability without overstating it.

risk.reason: These files do not implement the feature, but stale packaging or release notes can make the shipped interface diverge from the reviewed code.

branchCommit.subject: feat(virtual-commits): let humans review AI-sized diffs in reading order
branchCommit.author: Ne9roni

branchCommit.subject: feat(virtual-commits): let humans review AI-sized diffs in reading order

demo.hint: Overview · V1 docs → V6 release metadata · complete range = Real
